toolUseResult.originalFile:
#!/bin/bash
# common.sh - Shared library for Sidekick hooks system
#
# This file contains all shared functions used by Sidekick handlers and features.
# Functions are organized into namespaces for clarity:
#   - LOGGING: Session-specific logging with levels
#   - CONFIGURATION: Config cascade and feature toggles
#   - PATH RESOLUTION: Scope detection and path management
#   - JSON PROCESSING: JSON parsing and extraction
#   - PROCESS MANAGEMENT: Background process lifecycle
#   - CLAUDE INVOCATION: LLM invocation with isolation
#   - WORKSPACE MANAGEMENT: Temporary workspace creation
#   - UTILITIES: General-purpose helpers
#
# This file is sourced once per hook invocation and should never spawn
# subprocesses (except intentional background processes via process_launch_background).

# Double-source guard
if [ -n "${_SIDEKICK_COMMON_LOADED:-}" ]; then
    return 0
fi
readonly _SIDEKICK_COMMON_LOADED=1

# Strict mode
set -euo pipefail

# ============================================================================
# ANSI COLOR CONSTANTS
# ============================================================================
readonly COLOR_RESET='\033[0m'
readonly COLOR_RED='\033[0;31m'
readonly COLOR_GREEN='\033[0;32m'
readonly COLOR_YELLOW='\033[0;33m'
readonly COLOR_BLUE='\033[0;34m'
readonly COLOR_MAGENTA='\033[0;35m'
readonly COLOR_CYAN='\033[0;36m'
readonly COLOR_GRAY='\033[0;90m'
readonly COLOR_DIM='\033[2m'
readonly COLOR_BOLD='\033[1m'

# ============================================================================
# ERROR TRAP
# ============================================================================
error_trap() {
    local line_number="$1"
    local exit_code="$?"
    echo -e "${COLOR_RED}ERROR: Failed at line ${line_number} with exit code ${exit_code}${COLOR_RESET}" >&2
}
trap 'error_trap $LINENO' ERR

# ============================================================================
# GLOBAL STATE
# ============================================================================
# Session-specific log file (set by log_init)
_LOG_FILE=""

# Global log file (always available)
_GLOBAL_LOG_FILE=""

# Sidekick root directory (set by path_get_sidekick_root)
_SIDEKICK_ROOT=""

# ============================================================================
# LOGGING NAMESPACE
# ============================================================================

# Initialize logging with dual log support (global + session-specific)
# Sets up:
#   - Global log: ${CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR}/.sidekick/sidekick.log (always created)
#   - Session log: ${CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR}/.sidekick/sessions/${session_id}/sidekick.log (only if session dir exists)
# Args:
#   $1 - session_id (UUID format, optional - if empty only global log is used)
# Globals:
#   _GLOBAL_LOG_FILE - set to global log file path
#   _LOG_FILE - set to session log file path (empty if session dir doesn't exist)
log_init() {
    local session_id="${1:-}"

    # Initialize global log file (always create .sidekick directory)
    if [ -n "${CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR:-}" ]; then
        local sidekick_dir="${CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR}/.sidekick"
        mkdir -p "$sidekick_dir"
        _GLOBAL_LOG_FILE="${sidekick_dir}/sidekick.log"

        # Create global log file if it doesn't exist
        if [ ! -f "$_GLOBAL_LOG_FILE" ]; then
            touch "$_GLOBAL_LOG_FILE"
        fi
    else
        _GLOBAL_LOG_FILE=""
    fi

    # Initialize session-specific log file (only if session dir exists)
    if [ -n "$session_id" ] && [ -n "${CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR:-}" ]; then
        local session_dir="${CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR}/.sidekick/sessions/${session_id}"

        # Check if session directory exists (don't create it)
        if [ -d "$session_dir" ]; then
            _LOG_FILE="${session_dir}/sidekick.log"

            # Create session log file if it doesn't exist
            if [ ! -f "$_LOG_FILE" ]; then
                touch "$_LOG_FILE"
            fi
        else
            _LOG_FILE=""
        fi
    else
        _LOG_FILE=""
    fi
}

# Log debug message (only shown if LOG_LEVEL=debug)
# Args:
#   $* - message to log
log_debug() {
    local level="${LOG_LEVEL:-info}"
    if [ "$level" = "debug" ]; then
        _log_format_ansi "DEBUG" "$*" >&2
        _log_to_file "DEBUG" "$*"
    fi
}

# Log info message
# Args:
#   $* - message to log
log_info() {
    local level="${LOG_LEVEL:-info}"
    if [[ "$level" =~ ^(debug|info)$ ]]; then
        _log_format_ansi "INFO" "$*" >&2
        _log_to_file "INFO" "$*"
    fi
}

# Log warning message
# Args:
#   $* - message to log
log_warn() {
    local level="${LOG_LEVEL:-info}"
    if [[ "$level" =~ ^(debug|info|warn)$ ]]; then
        _log_format_ansi "WARN" "$*" >&2
        _log_to_file "WARN" "$*"
    fi
}

# Log error message
# Args:
#   $* - message to log
log_error() {
    _log_format_ansi "ERROR" "$*" >&2
    _log_to_file "ERROR" "$*"
}

# Internal: Write log entry to file(s)
# Writes to both global and session-specific logs (if available)
# Args:
#   $1 - log level
#   $2 - message
_log_to_file() {
    local level="$1"
    shift
    local message="$*"
    local timestamp
    timestamp=$(date '+%Y-%m-%d %H:%M:%S')
    local log_line="[${timestamp}] [${level}] ${message}"

    # Write to global log (always, if available)
    if [ -n "$_GLOBAL_LOG_FILE" ]; then
        echo "$log_line" >> "$_GLOBAL_LOG_FILE"
    fi

    # Write to session-specific log (only if session dir exists)
    if [ -n "$_LOG_FILE" ]; then
        echo "$log_line" >> "$_LOG_FILE"
    fi
}

# Internal: Format log message with ANSI colors
# Args:
#   $1 - log level
#   $2 - message
_log_format_ansi() {
    local level="$1"
    shift
    local message="$*"
    local color=""

    case "$level" in
        DEBUG) color="$COLOR_GRAY" ;;
        INFO)  color="$COLOR_GREEN" ;;
        WARN)  color="$COLOR_YELLOW" ;;
        ERROR) color="$COLOR_RED" ;;
        *)     color="$COLOR_RESET" ;;
    esac

    echo -e "${color}[${level}]${COLOR_RESET} ${message}"
}

# ============================================================================
# CONFIGURATION NAMESPACE
# ============================================================================

# Load configuration cascade: defaults → user → project
# Sources config files in order, later values override earlier ones
# Globals:
#   All config variables (FEATURE_*, TOPIC_*, SLEEPER_*, etc.)
config_load() {
    local sidekick_root
    sidekick_root=$(path_get_sidekick_root)

    # 1. Load defaults (must exist)
    local defaults="${sidekick_root}/config.defaults"
    if [ ! -f "$defaults" ]; then
        echo "ERROR: config.defaults not found at ${defaults}" >&2
        exit 2
    fi
    # shellcheck disable=SC1090
    source "$defaults"

    # 2. Load user config (optional)
    # Allow override for testing (e.g., SIDEKICK_USER_ROOT=sidekick-test)
    local user_dir="${SIDEKICK_USER_ROOT:-sidekick}"
    local user_config="${HOME}/.claude/hooks/${user_dir}/sidekick.conf"
    if [ -f "$user_config" ]; then
        # shellcheck disable=SC1090
        source "$user_config"
    fi

    # 3. Load project config (optional)
    if [ -n "${CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR:-}" ]; then
        local project_config="${CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR}/.claude/hooks/sidekick/sidekick.conf"
        if [ -f "$project_config" ]; then
            # shellcheck disable=SC1090
            source "$project_config"
        fi
    fi

    # Validate configuration
    _config_validate
}

# Get configuration value
# Args:
#   $1 - config key name
# Returns:
#   Config value or empty string
config_get() {
    local key="$1"
    echo "${!key:-}"
}

# Check if feature is enabled
# Args:
#   $1 - feature name (e.g., "topic_extraction", "resume", "tracking")
# Returns:
#   0 if enabled, 1 if disabled
config_is_feature_enabled() {
    local feature="$1"
    local key="FEATURE_${feature^^}"  # Convert to uppercase
    local value="${!key:-false}"

    if [ "$value" = "true" ]; then
        return 0
    else
        return 1
    fi
}

# Internal: Validate configuration values
# Checks for common misconfigurations and invalid values
_config_validate() {
    # Validate LOG_LEVEL
    local level="${LOG_LEVEL:-info}"
    if [[ ! "$level" =~ ^(debug|info|warn|error)$ ]]; then
        echo "WARNING: Invalid LOG_LEVEL '${level}', defaulting to 'info'" >&2
        LOG_LEVEL="info"
    fi

    # Validate numeric values are positive integers
    local numeric_vars=(
        "TOPIC_CADENCE_HIGH"
        "TOPIC_CADENCE_LOW"
        "TOPIC_CLARITY_THRESHOLD"
        "SLEEPER_INTERVAL_ACTIVE"
        "SLEEPER_INTERVAL_IDLE"
        "SLEEPER_MAX_DURATION"
        "SLEEPER_CLARITY_EXIT"
        "SLEEPER_MIN_SIZE_CHANGE"
        "SLEEPER_MIN_INTERVAL"
        "RESUME_MIN_CLARITY"
        "STATUSLINE_TOKEN_THRESHOLD"
        "TRACKING_STATIC_CADENCE"
        "CLEANUP_MIN_COUNT"
        "CLEANUP_AGE_DAYS"
    )

    for var in "${numeric_vars[@]}"; do
        local value="${!var:-0}"
        if ! [[ "$value" =~ ^[0-9]+$ ]]; then
            echo "WARNING: Invalid ${var} '${value}', must be positive integer" >&2
            return 1
        fi
    done

    return 0
}

# ============================================================================
# PATH RESOLUTION NAMESPACE
# ============================================================================

# Detect execution scope (user or project)
# Determines if running from ~/.claude or project .claude
# Returns:
#   "user" or "project"
path_detect_scope() {
    local script_path
    script_path=$(readlink -f "${BASH_SOURCE[1]}")

    if [[ "$script_path" == "${HOME}/.claude"* ]]; then
        echo "user"
    else
        echo "project"
    fi
}

# Get sidekick installation directory
# Returns absolute path to sidekick root (contains sidekick.sh)
# Globals:
#   _SIDEKICK_ROOT - cached value
path_get_sidekick_root() {
    # Return cached value if available
    if [ -n "$_SIDEKICK_ROOT" ]; then
        echo "$_SIDEKICK_ROOT"
        return 0
    fi

    # Determine root from this script's location
    # This file is at: {root}/lib/common.sh
    local script_dir
    script_dir=$(cd "$(dirname "${BASH_SOURCE[0]}")" && pwd)
    _SIDEKICK_ROOT=$(cd "${script_dir}/.." && pwd)

    echo "$_SIDEKICK_ROOT"
}

# Get session-specific directory (creates if missing)
# Args:
#   $1 - session_id (UUID format)
# Returns:
#   Absolute path to session directory: ${CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR}/.sidekick/sessions/{session-id}/
# Notes:
#   - CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR must be set (Sidekick requires project context)
#   - Session state is always stored at project root in .sidekick/sessions/
#   - This directory is gitignored and contains ephemeral session state
path_get_session_dir() {
    local session_id="$1"

    # Require CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR - Sidekick only works in project contexts
    if [ -z "${CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR:-}" ]; then
        log_error "CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR is not set. Sidekick requires a project context."
        return 1
    fi

    # Use project-local session directory
    local session_dir="${CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR}/.sidekick/sessions/${session_id}"

    # Create if doesn't exist
    if [ ! -d "$session_dir" ]; then
        mkdir -p "$session_dir"
    fi

    echo "$session_dir"
}

# Get project directory from JSON input or environment
# Args:
#   $1 - JSON input (optional, reads from stdin if empty)
# Returns:
#   Absolute path to project directory
path_get_project_dir() {
    local json="${1:-}"

    # If JSON provided, try to extract from it
    if [ -n "$json" ]; then
        local from_json
        from_json=$(json_get "$json" ".workspace.project_dir // .workspace.current_dir // empty")
        if [ -n "$from_json" ]; then
            _path_normalize "$from_json"
            return 0
        fi
    fi

    # Fall back to CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR environment variable
    if [ -n "${CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR:-}" ]; then
        _path_normalize "$CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR"
        return 0
    fi

    # Fall back to current directory
    pwd
}

# Internal: Normalize path (resolve symlinks, remove trailing slash)
# Args:
#   $1 - path to normalize
# Returns:
#   Normalized absolute path
_path_normalize() {
    local path="$1"

    # Resolve to absolute path
    if [ -e "$path" ]; then
        readlink -f "$path"
    else
        # Path doesn't exist, just clean it up
        echo "$path" | sed 's:/*$::'
    fi
}

# ============================================================================
# JSON PROCESSING NAMESPACE
# ============================================================================

# Extract value from JSON using jq
# Args:
#   $1 - JSON string
#   $2 - jq path (e.g., ".session_id", ".workspace.project_dir")
# Returns:
#   Extracted value or empty string
json_get() {
    local json="$1"
    local path="$2"

    echo "$json" | jq -r "${path} // empty" 2>/dev/null || echo ""
}

# Extract session_id from JSON
# Args:
#   $1 - JSON string
# Returns:
#   Session ID or empty string
json_get_session_id() {
    json_get "$1" ".session_id"
}

# Extract transcript_path from JSON
# Args:
#   $1 - JSON string
# Returns:
#   Transcript path or empty string
json_get_transcript_path() {
    json_get "$1" ".transcript_path"
}

# Validate JSON syntax
# Args:
#   $1 - JSON string
# Returns:
#   0 if valid, 1 if invalid
json_validate() {
    local json="$1"
    echo "$json" | jq empty 2>/dev/null
}

# Extract JSON from markdown code block
# Handles Claude output that wraps JSON in ```json...```
# Args:
#   $1 - text that may contain markdown-wrapped JSON
# Returns:
#   Extracted JSON or original text if not wrapped
json_extract_from_markdown() {
    local text="$1"

    # Check if wrapped in markdown code block
    if echo "$text" | grep -q '^```json'; then
        # Extract content between ```json and ```
        echo "$text" | sed -n '/^```json$/,/^```$/p' | sed '1d;$d'
    else
        # Return as-is
        echo "$text"
    fi
}

# ============================================================================
# PROCESS MANAGEMENT NAMESPACE
# ============================================================================

# Launch background process with PID tracking
# Args:
#   $1 - session_id
#   $2 - process name (e.g., "sleeper", "cleanup", "analysis")
#   $3 - function name to execute
#   $@ - additional arguments to pass to function
# Creates:
#   ${session_dir}/${name}.pid - PID file
#   ${session_dir}/${name}.log - Log file
process_launch_background() {
    local session_id="$1"
    local name="$2"
    local function="$3"
    shift 3
    local args=("$@")

    local session_dir
    session_dir=$(path_get_session_dir "$session_id")
    local pid_file="${session_dir}/${name}.pid"
    local log_file="${session_dir}/${name}.log"

    # Check if already running
    if process_is_running "$pid_file"; then
        log_debug "Process ${name} already running for session ${session_id}"
        return 0
    fi

    # Launch background process
    log_debug "Launching ${name} process for session ${session_id}"

    # Use nohup to detach from terminal and redirect output to log
    nohup bash -c "
        # Source common.sh to have access to all functions
        source '$(path_get_sidekick_root)/lib/common.sh'

        # Call the function with args
        $function ${args[*]} >> '$log_file' 2>&1
    " &

    local pid=$!
    echo "$pid" > "$pid_file"

    log_debug "Launched ${name} with PID ${pid}"
}

# Check if process is running by PID file
# Args:
#   $1 - PID file path
# Returns:
#   0 if running, 1 if not running
process_is_running() {
    local pid_file="$1"

    if [ ! -f "$pid_file" ]; then
        return 1
    fi

    local pid
    pid=$(cat "$pid_file")

    # Check if PID exists and is running
    if kill -0 "$pid" 2>/dev/null; then
        return 0
    else
        return 1
    fi
}

# Kill process by PID file (graceful then forceful)
# Args:
#   $1 - PID file path
process_kill() {
    local pid_file="$1"

    if [ ! -f "$pid_file" ]; then
        return 0
    fi

    local pid
    pid=$(cat "$pid_file")

    # Try graceful shutdown first
    if kill -0 "$pid" 2>/dev/null; then
        log_debug "Sending SIGTERM to PID ${pid}"
        kill -TERM "$pid" 2>/dev/null || true

        # Wait up to 5 seconds for graceful shutdown
        local waited=0
        while kill -0 "$pid" 2>/dev/null && [ $waited -lt 5 ]; do
            sleep 1
            waited=$((waited + 1))
        done

        # Force kill if still running
        if kill -0 "$pid" 2>/dev/null; then
            log_warn "Process ${pid} did not respond to SIGTERM, sending SIGKILL"
            kill -KILL "$pid" 2>/dev/null || true
        fi
    fi

    # Remove PID file
    rm -f "$pid_file"
}

# Clean up stale PID files (process no longer running)
# Args:
#   $1 - session directory path
process_cleanup_stale_pids() {
    local session_dir="$1"

    if [ ! -d "$session_dir" ]; then
        return 0
    fi

    # Find all .pid files
    local pid_file
    while IFS= read -r pid_file; do
        if [ -f "$pid_file" ]; then
            if ! process_is_running "$pid_file"; then
                log_debug "Cleaning up stale PID file: ${pid_file}"
                rm -f "$pid_file"
            fi
        fi
    done < <(find "$session_dir" -name "*.pid" 2>/dev/null)
}

# ============================================================================
# CLAUDE INVOCATION NAMESPACE
# ============================================================================

# Find Claude CLI binary
# Returns:
#   Path to claude binary
# Exits:
#   3 if claude binary not found
claude_find_bin() {
    # 1. Check CLAUDE_BIN config
    if [ -n "${CLAUDE_BIN:-}" ] && [ -x "$CLAUDE_BIN" ]; then
        echo "$CLAUDE_BIN"
        return 0
    fi

    # 2. Check ~/.claude/local/claude (default installation)
    local local_claude="${HOME}/.claude/local/claude"
    if [ -x "$local_claude" ]; then
        echo "$local_claude"
        return 0
    fi

    # 3. Check PATH using command -v
    local path_claude
    path_claude=$(command -v claude 2>/dev/null || true)
    if [ -n "$path_claude" ] && [ -x "$path_claude" ]; then
        echo "$path_claude"
        return 0
    fi

    # Not found
    echo "ERROR: Claude CLI binary not found" >&2
    echo "  Checked: CLAUDE_BIN, ~/.claude/local/claude, PATH" >&2
    exit 3
}

# Invoke Claude with isolation and error handling
# Args:
#   $1 - model name (e.g., "haiku-4.5", "haiku-3.5")
#   $2 - prompt text
#   $3 - timeout in seconds (optional, default: 30)
# Returns:
#   JSON output (extracted from markdown if needed)
# Exits:
#   1 on error
claude_invoke() {
    local model="$1"
    local prompt="$2"
    local timeout="${3:-30}"

    local claude_bin
    claude_bin=$(claude_find_bin)

    # Create isolated workspace to prevent hook recursion
    local workspace
    workspace=$(workspace_create "claude-invoke-$$")

    log_debug "Invoking Claude model ${model} with timeout ${timeout}s in workspace ${workspace}"
    log_debug "Using Claude binary: ${claude_bin}"

    # Change to workspace directory and invoke Claude using project settings
    # This prevents hook recursion by using isolated .claude/settings.json
    local output
    local claude_errors
    local claude_output_file
    claude_errors=$(mktemp)
    claude_output_file=$(mktemp)

    # Use heredoc to pass prompt, write to temp file to avoid subshell issues
    # Disable exit-on-error temporarily to capture exit code
    set +e
    (cd "$workspace" && timeout "$timeout" "$claude_bin" -p \
        --model "$model" \
        --setting-sources project \
        <<EOF
$prompt
EOF
    ) > "$claude_output_file" 2>"$claude_errors"
    exit_code=$?
    set -e

    # Read output from file
    output=$(cat "$claude_output_file")
    rm -f "$claude_output_file"

    # Check if Claude invocation failed
    if [ $exit_code -ne 0 ]; then
        errors=$(cat "$claude_errors" 2>/dev/null || echo "")
        log_error "Claude invocation failed (exit code: ${exit_code})"
        log_error "Command: cd $workspace && $claude_bin -p --model $model --setting-sources project"
        log_error "Stderr: ${errors}"
        log_error "Stdout: ${output}"
        rm -f "$claude_errors"
        workspace_cleanup "$workspace"
        return 1
    fi

    # Check for stderr warnings
    if [ -s "$claude_errors" ]; then
        log_warn "Claude stderr: $(cat "$claude_errors")"
    fi
    rm -f "$claude_errors"

    # Cleanup workspace
    workspace_cleanup "$workspace"

    # Extract JSON from output (handle markdown wrapping)
    local json
    json=$(claude_extract_json "$output")

    # Validate JSON
    if ! json_validate "$json"; then
        log_error "Claude output is not valid JSON: $json"
        return 1
    fi

    echo "$json"
}

# Extract JSON from Claude output (handles markdown wrapping)
# Args:
#   $1 - Claude output text
# Returns:
#   Extracted JSON
claude_extract_json() {
    local output="$1"

    # Try to extract from markdown code block first
    local extracted
    extracted=$(json_extract_from_markdown "$output")

    # If extraction changed the output, use it
    if [ "$extracted" != "$output" ]; then
        echo "$extracted"
        return 0
    fi

    # Otherwise, look for JSON object in output
    # Match from first { to last }
    if echo "$output" | grep -q '{'; then
        echo "$output" | sed -n '/{/,/}/p'
    else
        echo "$output"
    fi
}

# ============================================================================
# WORKSPACE MANAGEMENT NAMESPACE
# ============================================================================

# Create isolated workspace (prevents hook recursion)
# Args:
#   $1 - identifier (e.g., "claude-invoke-12345")
# Returns:
#   Path to workspace directory
workspace_create() {
    local identifier="$1"
    local workspace="/tmp/sidekick-${identifier}"

    # Create workspace directory with .claude subdirectory
    mkdir -p "${workspace}/.claude"

    # Create isolated settings.json with hooks disabled
    # This prevents hook recursion when Claude is invoked from within a hook
    cat > "${workspace}/.claude/settings.json" <<'EOF'
{
  "hooks": {},
  "statusline": {
    "enabled": false
  }
}
EOF

    log_debug "Created isolated workspace: ${workspace}"
    echo "$workspace"
}

# Cleanup workspace
# Args:
#   $1 - workspace path
workspace_cleanup() {
    local workspace="$1"

    if [ -d "$workspace" ]; then
        log_debug "Cleaning up workspace: ${workspace}"
        rm -rf "$workspace"
    fi
}

# ============================================================================
# UTILITIES NAMESPACE
# ============================================================================

# Validate that value is non-negative integer
# Args:
#   $1 - value to validate
# Returns:
#   0 if valid, 1 if invalid
util_validate_count() {
    local value="$1"

    if [[ "$value" =~ ^[0-9]+$ ]]; then
        return 0
    else
        return 1
    fi
}

# Get file size (cross-platform)
# Args:
#   $1 - file path
# Returns:
#   File size in bytes
util_get_file_size() {
    local file="$1"

    if [ ! -f "$file" ]; then
        echo "0"
        return 0
    fi

    # Try stat (GNU/Linux)
    if stat --version &>/dev/null; then
        stat -c %s "$file" 2>/dev/null || echo "0"
    else
        # Try BSD stat (macOS)
        stat -f %z "$file" 2>/dev/null || echo "0"
    fi
}

# Create session directory if missing
# Args:
#   $1 - session_id
util_create_session_dir() {
    local session_id="$1"
    path_get_session_dir "$session_id" >/dev/null
}

# Calculate tokens from transcript (rough estimate)
# Uses approximation: 1 token ≈ 4 characters for English text
# Args:
#   $1 - transcript file path
# Returns:
#   Estimated token count
util_calculate_tokens() {
    local transcript="$1"

    if [ ! -f "$transcript" ]; then
        echo "0"
        return 0
    fi

    # Count characters and divide by 4
    local chars
    chars=$(wc -c < "$transcript" 2>/dev/null || echo "0")
    echo $((chars / 4))
}

toolUseResult.originalFile:
#!/bin/bash
# common.sh - Shared library for Sidekick hooks system
#
# This file contains all shared functions used by Sidekick handlers and features.
# Functions are organized into namespaces for clarity:
#   - LOGGING: Session-specific logging with levels
#   - CONFIGURATION: Config cascade and feature toggles
#   - PATH RESOLUTION: Scope detection and path management
#   - JSON PROCESSING: JSON parsing and extraction
#   - PROCESS MANAGEMENT: Background process lifecycle
#   - CLAUDE INVOCATION: LLM invocation with isolation
#   - WORKSPACE MANAGEMENT: Temporary workspace creation
#   - UTILITIES: General-purpose helpers
#
# This file is sourced once per hook invocation and should never spawn
# subprocesses (except intentional background processes via process_launch_background).

# Double-source guard
if [ -n "${_SIDEKICK_COMMON_LOADED:-}" ]; then
    return 0
fi
readonly _SIDEKICK_COMMON_LOADED=1

# Strict mode
set -euo pipefail

# ============================================================================
# ANSI COLOR CONSTANTS
# ============================================================================
readonly COLOR_RESET='\033[0m'
readonly COLOR_RED='\033[0;31m'
readonly COLOR_GREEN='\033[0;32m'
readonly COLOR_YELLOW='\033[0;33m'
readonly COLOR_BLUE='\033[0;34m'
readonly COLOR_MAGENTA='\033[0;35m'
readonly COLOR_CYAN='\033[0;36m'
readonly COLOR_GRAY='\033[0;90m'
readonly COLOR_DIM='\033[2m'
readonly COLOR_BOLD='\033[1m'

# ============================================================================
# ERROR TRAP
# ============================================================================
error_trap() {
    local line_number="$1"
    local exit_code="$?"
    echo -e "${COLOR_RED}ERROR: Failed at line ${line_number} with exit code ${exit_code}${COLOR_RESET}" >&2
}
trap 'error_trap $LINENO' ERR

# ============================================================================
# GLOBAL STATE
# ============================================================================
# Session-specific log file (set by log_init)
_LOG_FILE=""

# Global log file (always available)
_GLOBAL_LOG_FILE=""

# Sidekick root directory (set by path_get_sidekick_root)
_SIDEKICK_ROOT=""

# ============================================================================
# LOGGING NAMESPACE
# ============================================================================

# Initialize logging with dual log support (global + session-specific)
# Sets up:
#   - Global log: ${CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR}/.sidekick/sidekick.log (always created)
#   - Session log: ${CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR}/.sidekick/sessions/${session_id}/sidekick.log (only if session dir exists)
# Args:
#   $1 - session_id (UUID format, optional - if empty only global log is used)
# Globals:
#   _GLOBAL_LOG_FILE - set to global log file path
#   _LOG_FILE - set to session log file path (empty if session dir doesn't exist)
log_init() {
    local session_id="${1:-}"

    # Initialize global log file (always create .sidekick directory)
    if [ -n "${CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR:-}" ]; then
        local sidekick_dir="${CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR}/.sidekick"
        mkdir -p "$sidekick_dir"
        _GLOBAL_LOG_FILE="${sidekick_dir}/sidekick.log"

        # Create global log file if it doesn't exist
        if [ ! -f "$_GLOBAL_LOG_FILE" ]; then
            touch "$_GLOBAL_LOG_FILE"
        fi
    else
        _GLOBAL_LOG_FILE=""
    fi

    # Initialize session-specific log file (only if session dir exists)
    if [ -n "$session_id" ] && [ -n "${CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR:-}" ]; then
        local session_dir="${CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR}/.sidekick/sessions/${session_id}"

        # Check if session directory exists (don't create it)
        if [ -d "$session_dir" ]; then
            _LOG_FILE="${session_dir}/sidekick.log"

            # Create session log file if it doesn't exist
            if [ ! -f "$_LOG_FILE" ]; then
                touch "$_LOG_FILE"
            fi
        else
            _LOG_FILE=""
        fi
    else
        _LOG_FILE=""
    fi
}

# Log debug message (only shown if LOG_LEVEL=debug)
# Args:
#   $* - message to log
log_debug() {
    local level="${LOG_LEVEL:-info}"
    if [ "$level" = "debug" ]; then
        _log_format_ansi "DEBUG" "$*" >&2
        _log_to_file "DEBUG" "$*"
    fi
}

# Log info message
# Args:
#   $* - message to log
log_info() {
    local level="${LOG_LEVEL:-info}"
    if [[ "$level" =~ ^(debug|info)$ ]]; then
        _log_format_ansi "INFO" "$*" >&2
        _log_to_file "INFO" "$*"
    fi
}

# Log warning message
# Args:
#   $* - message to log
log_warn() {
    local level="${LOG_LEVEL:-info}"
    if [[ "$level" =~ ^(debug|info|warn)$ ]]; then
        _log_format_ansi "WARN" "$*" >&2
        _log_to_file "WARN" "$*"
    fi
}

# Log error message
# Args:
#   $* - message to log
log_error() {
    _log_format_ansi "ERROR" "$*" >&2
    _log_to_file "ERROR" "$*"
}

# Internal: Write log entry to file(s)
# Writes to both global and session-specific logs (if available)
# Args:
#   $1 - log level
#   $2 - message
_log_to_file() {
    local level="$1"
    shift
    local message="$*"
    local timestamp
    timestamp=$(date '+%Y-%m-%d %H:%M:%S')
    local log_line="[${timestamp}] [${level}] ${message}"

    # Write to global log (always, if available)
    if [ -n "$_GLOBAL_LOG_FILE" ]; then
        echo "$log_line" >> "$_GLOBAL_LOG_FILE"
    fi

    # Write to session-specific log (only if session dir exists)
    if [ -n "$_LOG_FILE" ]; then
        echo "$log_line" >> "$_LOG_FILE"
    fi
}

# Internal: Format log message with ANSI colors
# Args:
#   $1 - log level
#   $2 - message
_log_format_ansi() {
    local level="$1"
    shift
    local message="$*"
    local color=""

    case "$level" in
        DEBUG) color="$COLOR_GRAY" ;;
        INFO)  color="$COLOR_GREEN" ;;
        WARN)  color="$COLOR_YELLOW" ;;
        ERROR) color="$COLOR_RED" ;;
        *)     color="$COLOR_RESET" ;;
    esac

    echo -e "${color}[${level}]${COLOR_RESET} ${message}"
}

# ============================================================================
# CONFIGURATION NAMESPACE
# ============================================================================

# Load configuration cascade: defaults → user → project
# Sources config files in order, later values override earlier ones
# Globals:
#   All config variables (FEATURE_*, TOPIC_*, SLEEPER_*, etc.)
config_load() {
    local sidekick_root
    sidekick_root=$(path_get_sidekick_root)

    # 1. Load defaults (must exist)
    local defaults="${sidekick_root}/config.defaults"
    if [ ! -f "$defaults" ]; then
        echo "ERROR: config.defaults not found at ${defaults}" >&2
        exit 2
    fi
    # shellcheck disable=SC1090
    source "$defaults"

    # 2. Load user config (optional)
    # Allow override for testing (e.g., SIDEKICK_USER_ROOT=sidekick-test)
    local user_dir="${SIDEKICK_USER_ROOT:-sidekick}"
    local user_config="${HOME}/.claude/hooks/${user_dir}/sidekick.conf"
    if [ -f "$user_config" ]; then
        # shellcheck disable=SC1090
        source "$user_config"
    fi

    # 3. Load project config (optional)
    if [ -n "${CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR:-}" ]; then
        local project_config="${CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR}/.claude/hooks/sidekick/sidekick.conf"
        if [ -f "$project_config" ]; then
            # shellcheck disable=SC1090
            source "$project_config"
        fi
    fi

    # Validate configuration
    _config_validate
}

# Get configuration value
# Args:
#   $1 - config key name
# Returns:
#   Config value or empty string
config_get() {
    local key="$1"
    echo "${!key:-}"
}

# Check if feature is enabled
# Args:
#   $1 - feature name (e.g., "topic_extraction", "resume", "tracking")
# Returns:
#   0 if enabled, 1 if disabled
config_is_feature_enabled() {
    local feature="$1"
    local key="FEATURE_${feature^^}"  # Convert to uppercase
    local value="${!key:-false}"

    if [ "$value" = "true" ]; then
        return 0
    else
        return 1
    fi
}

# Internal: Validate configuration values
# Checks for common misconfigurations and invalid values
_config_validate() {
    # Validate LOG_LEVEL
    local level="${LOG_LEVEL:-info}"
    if [[ ! "$level" =~ ^(debug|info|warn|error)$ ]]; then
        echo "WARNING: Invalid LOG_LEVEL '${level}', defaulting to 'info'" >&2
        LOG_LEVEL="info"
    fi

    # Validate numeric values are positive integers
    local numeric_vars=(
        "TOPIC_CADENCE_HIGH"
        "TOPIC_CADENCE_LOW"
        "TOPIC_CLARITY_THRESHOLD"
        "SLEEPER_INTERVAL_ACTIVE"
        "SLEEPER_INTERVAL_IDLE"
        "SLEEPER_MAX_DURATION"
        "SLEEPER_CLARITY_EXIT"
        "SLEEPER_MIN_SIZE_CHANGE"
        "SLEEPER_MIN_INTERVAL"
        "RESUME_MIN_CLARITY"
        "STATUSLINE_TOKEN_THRESHOLD"
        "TRACKING_STATIC_CADENCE"
        "CLEANUP_MIN_COUNT"
        "CLEANUP_AGE_DAYS"
    )

    for var in "${numeric_vars[@]}"; do
        local value="${!var:-0}"
        if ! [[ "$value" =~ ^[0-9]+$ ]]; then
            echo "WARNING: Invalid ${var} '${value}', must be positive integer" >&2
            return 1
        fi
    done

    return 0
}

# ============================================================================
# PATH RESOLUTION NAMESPACE
# ============================================================================

# Detect execution scope (user or project)
# Determines if running from ~/.claude or project .claude
# Returns:
#   "user" or "project"
path_detect_scope() {
    local script_path
    script_path=$(readlink -f "${BASH_SOURCE[1]}")

    if [[ "$script_path" == "${HOME}/.claude"* ]]; then
        echo "user"
    else
        echo "project"
    fi
}

# Get sidekick installation directory
# Returns absolute path to sidekick root (contains sidekick.sh)
# Globals:
#   _SIDEKICK_ROOT - cached value
path_get_sidekick_root() {
    # Return cached value if available
    if [ -n "$_SIDEKICK_ROOT" ]; then
        echo "$_SIDEKICK_ROOT"
        return 0
    fi

    # Determine root from this script's location
    # This file is at: {root}/lib/common.sh
    local script_dir
    script_dir=$(cd "$(dirname "${BASH_SOURCE[0]}")" && pwd)
    _SIDEKICK_ROOT=$(cd "${script_dir}/.." && pwd)

    echo "$_SIDEKICK_ROOT"
}

# Get session-specific directory path (does NOT create directory)
# Args:
#   $1 - session_id (UUID format)
# Returns:
#   Absolute path to session directory: ${CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR}/.sidekick/sessions/{session-id}/
# Notes:
#   - CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR must be set (Sidekick requires project context)
#   - Session state is always stored at project root in .sidekick/sessions/
#   - This directory is gitignored and contains ephemeral session state
#   - This function does NOT create the directory - use util_create_session_dir() for that
path_get_session_dir() {
    local session_id="$1"

    # Require CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR - Sidekick only works in project contexts
    if [ -z "${CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR:-}" ]; then
        log_error "CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR is not set. Sidekick requires a project context."
        return 1
    fi

    # Return project-local session directory path
    echo "${CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR}/.sidekick/sessions/${session_id}"
}

# Get project directory from JSON input or environment
# Args:
#   $1 - JSON input (optional, reads from stdin if empty)
# Returns:
#   Absolute path to project directory
path_get_project_dir() {
    local json="${1:-}"

    # If JSON provided, try to extract from it
    if [ -n "$json" ]; then
        local from_json
        from_json=$(json_get "$json" ".workspace.project_dir // .workspace.current_dir // empty")
        if [ -n "$from_json" ]; then
            _path_normalize "$from_json"
            return 0
        fi
    fi

    # Fall back to CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR environment variable
    if [ -n "${CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR:-}" ]; then
        _path_normalize "$CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR"
        return 0
    fi

    # Fall back to current directory
    pwd
}

# Internal: Normalize path (resolve symlinks, remove trailing slash)
# Args:
#   $1 - path to normalize
# Returns:
#   Normalized absolute path
_path_normalize() {
    local path="$1"

    # Resolve to absolute path
    if [ -e "$path" ]; then
        readlink -f "$path"
    else
        # Path doesn't exist, just clean it up
        echo "$path" | sed 's:/*$::'
    fi
}

# ============================================================================
# JSON PROCESSING NAMESPACE
# ============================================================================

# Extract value from JSON using jq
# Args:
#   $1 - JSON string
#   $2 - jq path (e.g., ".session_id", ".workspace.project_dir")
# Returns:
#   Extracted value or empty string
json_get() {
    local json="$1"
    local path="$2"

    echo "$json" | jq -r "${path} // empty" 2>/dev/null || echo ""
}

# Extract session_id from JSON
# Args:
#   $1 - JSON string
# Returns:
#   Session ID or empty string
json_get_session_id() {
    json_get "$1" ".session_id"
}

# Extract transcript_path from JSON
# Args:
#   $1 - JSON string
# Returns:
#   Transcript path or empty string
json_get_transcript_path() {
    json_get "$1" ".transcript_path"
}

# Validate JSON syntax
# Args:
#   $1 - JSON string
# Returns:
#   0 if valid, 1 if invalid
json_validate() {
    local json="$1"
    echo "$json" | jq empty 2>/dev/null
}

# Extract JSON from markdown code block
# Handles Claude output that wraps JSON in ```json...```
# Args:
#   $1 - text that may contain markdown-wrapped JSON
# Returns:
#   Extracted JSON or original text if not wrapped
json_extract_from_markdown() {
    local text="$1"

    # Check if wrapped in markdown code block
    if echo "$text" | grep -q '^```json'; then
        # Extract content between ```json and ```
        echo "$text" | sed -n '/^```json$/,/^```$/p' | sed '1d;$d'
    else
        # Return as-is
        echo "$text"
    fi
}

# ============================================================================
# PROCESS MANAGEMENT NAMESPACE
# ============================================================================

# Launch background process with PID tracking
# Args:
#   $1 - session_id
#   $2 - process name (e.g., "sleeper", "cleanup", "analysis")
#   $3 - function name to execute
#   $@ - additional arguments to pass to function
# Creates:
#   ${session_dir}/${name}.pid - PID file
#   ${session_dir}/${name}.log - Log file
process_launch_background() {
    local session_id="$1"
    local name="$2"
    local function="$3"
    shift 3
    local args=("$@")

    local session_dir
    session_dir=$(path_get_session_dir "$session_id")
    local pid_file="${session_dir}/${name}.pid"
    local log_file="${session_dir}/${name}.log"

    # Check if already running
    if process_is_running "$pid_file"; then
        log_debug "Process ${name} already running for session ${session_id}"
        return 0
    fi

    # Launch background process
    log_debug "Launching ${name} process for session ${session_id}"

    # Use nohup to detach from terminal and redirect output to log
    nohup bash -c "
        # Source common.sh to have access to all functions
        source '$(path_get_sidekick_root)/lib/common.sh'

        # Call the function with args
        $function ${args[*]} >> '$log_file' 2>&1
    " &

    local pid=$!
    echo "$pid" > "$pid_file"

    log_debug "Launched ${name} with PID ${pid}"
}

# Check if process is running by PID file
# Args:
#   $1 - PID file path
# Returns:
#   0 if running, 1 if not running
process_is_running() {
    local pid_file="$1"

    if [ ! -f "$pid_file" ]; then
        return 1
    fi

    local pid
    pid=$(cat "$pid_file")

    # Check if PID exists and is running
    if kill -0 "$pid" 2>/dev/null; then
        return 0
    else
        return 1
    fi
}

# Kill process by PID file (graceful then forceful)
# Args:
#   $1 - PID file path
process_kill() {
    local pid_file="$1"

    if [ ! -f "$pid_file" ]; then
        return 0
    fi

    local pid
    pid=$(cat "$pid_file")

    # Try graceful shutdown first
    if kill -0 "$pid" 2>/dev/null; then
        log_debug "Sending SIGTERM to PID ${pid}"
        kill -TERM "$pid" 2>/dev/null || true

        # Wait up to 5 seconds for graceful shutdown
        local waited=0
        while kill -0 "$pid" 2>/dev/null && [ $waited -lt 5 ]; do
            sleep 1
            waited=$((waited + 1))
        done

        # Force kill if still running
        if kill -0 "$pid" 2>/dev/null; then
            log_warn "Process ${pid} did not respond to SIGTERM, sending SIGKILL"
            kill -KILL "$pid" 2>/dev/null || true
        fi
    fi

    # Remove PID file
    rm -f "$pid_file"
}

# Clean up stale PID files (process no longer running)
# Args:
#   $1 - session directory path
process_cleanup_stale_pids() {
    local session_dir="$1"

    if [ ! -d "$session_dir" ]; then
        return 0
    fi

    # Find all .pid files
    local pid_file
    while IFS= read -r pid_file; do
        if [ -f "$pid_file" ]; then
            if ! process_is_running "$pid_file"; then
                log_debug "Cleaning up stale PID file: ${pid_file}"
                rm -f "$pid_file"
            fi
        fi
    done < <(find "$session_dir" -name "*.pid" 2>/dev/null)
}

# ============================================================================
# CLAUDE INVOCATION NAMESPACE
# ============================================================================

# Find Claude CLI binary
# Returns:
#   Path to claude binary
# Exits:
#   3 if claude binary not found
claude_find_bin() {
    # 1. Check CLAUDE_BIN config
    if [ -n "${CLAUDE_BIN:-}" ] && [ -x "$CLAUDE_BIN" ]; then
        echo "$CLAUDE_BIN"
        return 0
    fi

    # 2. Check ~/.claude/local/claude (default installation)
    local local_claude="${HOME}/.claude/local/claude"
    if [ -x "$local_claude" ]; then
        echo "$local_claude"
        return 0
    fi

    # 3. Check PATH using command -v
    local path_claude
    path_claude=$(command -v claude 2>/dev/null || true)
    if [ -n "$path_claude" ] && [ -x "$path_claude" ]; then
        echo "$path_claude"
        return 0
    fi

    # Not found
    echo "ERROR: Claude CLI binary not found" >&2
    echo "  Checked: CLAUDE_BIN, ~/.claude/local/claude, PATH" >&2
    exit 3
}

# Invoke Claude with isolation and error handling
# Args:
#   $1 - model name (e.g., "haiku-4.5", "haiku-3.5")
#   $2 - prompt text
#   $3 - timeout in seconds (optional, default: 30)
# Returns:
#   JSON output (extracted from markdown if needed)
# Exits:
#   1 on error
claude_invoke() {
    local model="$1"
    local prompt="$2"
    local timeout="${3:-30}"

    local claude_bin
    claude_bin=$(claude_find_bin)

    # Create isolated workspace to prevent hook recursion
    local workspace
    workspace=$(workspace_create "claude-invoke-$$")

    log_debug "Invoking Claude model ${model} with timeout ${timeout}s in workspace ${workspace}"
    log_debug "Using Claude binary: ${claude_bin}"

    # Change to workspace directory and invoke Claude using project settings
    # This prevents hook recursion by using isolated .claude/settings.json
    local output
    local claude_errors
    local claude_output_file
    claude_errors=$(mktemp)
    claude_output_file=$(mktemp)

    # Use heredoc to pass prompt, write to temp file to avoid subshell issues
    # Disable exit-on-error temporarily to capture exit code
    set +e
    (cd "$workspace" && timeout "$timeout" "$claude_bin" -p \
        --model "$model" \
        --setting-sources project \
        <<EOF
$prompt
EOF
    ) > "$claude_output_file" 2>"$claude_errors"
    exit_code=$?
    set -e

    # Read output from file
    output=$(cat "$claude_output_file")
    rm -f "$claude_output_file"

    # Check if Claude invocation failed
    if [ $exit_code -ne 0 ]; then
        errors=$(cat "$claude_errors" 2>/dev/null || echo "")
        log_error "Claude invocation failed (exit code: ${exit_code})"
        log_error "Command: cd $workspace && $claude_bin -p --model $model --setting-sources project"
        log_error "Stderr: ${errors}"
        log_error "Stdout: ${output}"
        rm -f "$claude_errors"
        workspace_cleanup "$workspace"
        return 1
    fi

    # Check for stderr warnings
    if [ -s "$claude_errors" ]; then
        log_warn "Claude stderr: $(cat "$claude_errors")"
    fi
    rm -f "$claude_errors"

    # Cleanup workspace
    workspace_cleanup "$workspace"

    # Extract JSON from output (handle markdown wrapping)
    local json
    json=$(claude_extract_json "$output")

    # Validate JSON
    if ! json_validate "$json"; then
        log_error "Claude output is not valid JSON: $json"
        return 1
    fi

    echo "$json"
}

# Extract JSON from Claude output (handles markdown wrapping)
# Args:
#   $1 - Claude output text
# Returns:
#   Extracted JSON
claude_extract_json() {
    local output="$1"

    # Try to extract from markdown code block first
    local extracted
    extracted=$(json_extract_from_markdown "$output")

    # If extraction changed the output, use it
    if [ "$extracted" != "$output" ]; then
        echo "$extracted"
        return 0
    fi

    # Otherwise, look for JSON object in output
    # Match from first { to last }
    if echo "$output" | grep -q '{'; then
        echo "$output" | sed -n '/{/,/}/p'
    else
        echo "$output"
    fi
}

# ============================================================================
# WORKSPACE MANAGEMENT NAMESPACE
# ============================================================================

# Create isolated workspace (prevents hook recursion)
# Args:
#   $1 - identifier (e.g., "claude-invoke-12345")
# Returns:
#   Path to workspace directory
workspace_create() {
    local identifier="$1"
    local workspace="/tmp/sidekick-${identifier}"

    # Create workspace directory with .claude subdirectory
    mkdir -p "${workspace}/.claude"

    # Create isolated settings.json with hooks disabled
    # This prevents hook recursion when Claude is invoked from within a hook
    cat > "${workspace}/.claude/settings.json" <<'EOF'
{
  "hooks": {},
  "statusline": {
    "enabled": false
  }
}
EOF

    log_debug "Created isolated workspace: ${workspace}"
    echo "$workspace"
}

# Cleanup workspace
# Args:
#   $1 - workspace path
workspace_cleanup() {
    local workspace="$1"

    if [ -d "$workspace" ]; then
        log_debug "Cleaning up workspace: ${workspace}"
        rm -rf "$workspace"
    fi
}

# ============================================================================
# UTILITIES NAMESPACE
# ============================================================================

# Validate that value is non-negative integer
# Args:
#   $1 - value to validate
# Returns:
#   0 if valid, 1 if invalid
util_validate_count() {
    local value="$1"

    if [[ "$value" =~ ^[0-9]+$ ]]; then
        return 0
    else
        return 1
    fi
}

# Get file size (cross-platform)
# Args:
#   $1 - file path
# Returns:
#   File size in bytes
util_get_file_size() {
    local file="$1"

    if [ ! -f "$file" ]; then
        echo "0"
        return 0
    fi

    # Try stat (GNU/Linux)
    if stat --version &>/dev/null; then
        stat -c %s "$file" 2>/dev/null || echo "0"
    else
        # Try BSD stat (macOS)
        stat -f %z "$file" 2>/dev/null || echo "0"
    fi
}

# Create session directory if missing
# Args:
#   $1 - session_id
util_create_session_dir() {
    local session_id="$1"
    path_get_session_dir "$session_id" >/dev/null
}

# Calculate tokens from transcript (rough estimate)
# Uses approximation: 1 token ≈ 4 characters for English text
# Args:
#   $1 - transcript file path
# Returns:
#   Estimated token count
util_calculate_tokens() {
    local transcript="$1"

    if [ ! -f "$transcript" ]; then
        echo "0"
        return 0
    fi

    # Count characters and divide by 4
    local chars
    chars=$(wc -c < "$transcript" 2>/dev/null || echo "0")
    echo $((chars / 4))
}

#!/bin/bash
# Sidekick Feature: Tracking
# Request counting and static reminder injection

# Prevent double-sourcing
[[ -n "${_SIDEKICK_FEATURE_TRACKING_LOADED:-}" ]] && return 0
readonly _SIDEKICK_FEATURE_TRACKING_LOADED=1

# This file should be sourced, not executed
if [ "${BASH_SOURCE[0]}" -ef "$0" ]; then
    echo "ERROR: This file should be sourced, not executed" >&2
    exit 1
fi

# Requires lib/common.sh to be loaded
if [[ -z "${_SIDEKICK_COMMON_LOADED:-}" ]]; then
    echo "ERROR: lib/common.sh must be loaded before tracking.sh" >&2
    exit 1
fi

#------------------------------------------------------------------------------
# tracking_init - Initialize tracking counter for a session
#
# Creates the response_count file and initializes it to 0
#
# Arguments:
#   $1 - session_id
#
# Returns:
#   0 on success, 1 on failure
#------------------------------------------------------------------------------
tracking_init() {
    local session_id="$1"

    if [ -z "$session_id" ]; then
        log_error "tracking_init: session_id is required"
        return 1
    fi

    local session_dir
    session_dir=$(path_get_session_dir "$session_id")

    local counter_file="$session_dir/response_count"

    # Initialize counter to 0
    echo "0" > "$counter_file"

    log_debug "Tracking initialized: $counter_file"
    return 0
}

#------------------------------------------------------------------------------
# tracking_increment - Increment counter and return new value
#
# Atomically increments the counter and returns the new value
#
# Arguments:
#   $1 - session_id
#
# Returns:
#   Prints the new counter value to stdout
#   Exit code: 0 on success, 1 on failure
#------------------------------------------------------------------------------
tracking_increment() {
    local session_id="$1"

    if [ -z "$session_id" ]; then
        log_error "tracking_increment: session_id is required"
        return 1
    fi

    local session_dir
    session_dir=$(path_get_session_dir "$session_id")

    local counter_file="$session_dir/response_count"

    # Check if counter file exists
    if [ ! -f "$counter_file" ]; then
        log_warn "Counter file not found, initializing: $counter_file"
        tracking_init "$session_id" || return 1
    fi

    # Read current value
    local current
    current=$(cat "$counter_file" 2>/dev/null || echo "0")

    # Validate it's a number
    if ! util_validate_count "$current"; then
        log_error "Invalid counter value in $counter_file: $current"
        return 1
    fi

    # Increment
    local new_value=$((current + 1))

    # Write back
    echo "$new_value" > "$counter_file"

    log_debug "Tracking incremented: $current -> $new_value"

    # Output new value
    echo "$new_value"
    return 0
}

#------------------------------------------------------------------------------
# tracking_get - Get current counter value
#
# Reads and returns the current counter value without incrementing
#
# Arguments:
#   $1 - session_id
#
# Returns:
#   Prints the current counter value to stdout (0 if not initialized)
#   Exit code: 0 on success, 1 on failure
#------------------------------------------------------------------------------
tracking_get() {
    local session_id="$1"

    if [ -z "$session_id" ]; then
        log_error "tracking_get: session_id is required"
        return 1
    fi

    local session_dir
    session_dir=$(path_get_session_dir "$session_id")

    local counter_file="$session_dir/response_count"

    # Check if counter file exists
    if [ ! -f "$counter_file" ]; then
        echo "0"
        return 0
    fi

    # Read current value
    local current
    current=$(cat "$counter_file" 2>/dev/null || echo "0")

    # Validate it's a number
    if ! util_validate_count "$current"; then
        log_error "Invalid counter value in $counter_file: $current"
        return 1
    fi

    echo "$current"
    return 0
}

#------------------------------------------------------------------------------
# tracking_check_reminder - Check if static reminder is due
#
# Checks if we should output a static reminder based on the counter value
# and configured cadence. Loads static reminder text from files.
#
# Arguments:
#   $1 - count (current response count)
#   $2 - project_dir (optional, for project-specific reminders)
#
# Returns:
#   Prints reminder text to stdout if due, empty if not
#   Exit code: 0 always
#------------------------------------------------------------------------------
tracking_check_reminder() {
    local count="$1"
    local project_dir="${2:-}"

    # Get cadence from config (default to 4)
    local cadence
    cadence=$(config_get "TRACKING_STATIC_CADENCE")
    [ -z "$cadence" ] && cadence=4

    # Check if reminder is due (count is multiple of cadence, but not 0)
    if [ "$count" -gt 0 ] && [ $((count % cadence)) -eq 0 ]; then
        log_debug "Static reminder due at count $count (cadence $cadence)"

        # Try to load reminder text from files (project overrides user)
        local reminder_text=""

        # Try project-specific reminder first
        if [ -n "$project_dir" ]; then
            local project_reminder="$project_dir/.claude/hooks/sidekick/static-reminder.txt"
            if [ -f "$project_reminder" ]; then
                reminder_text=$(cat "$project_reminder")
                log_debug "Loaded project reminder from $project_reminder"
            fi
        fi

        # Fall back to user reminder if no project reminder
        if [ -z "$reminder_text" ]; then
            local sidekick_root
            sidekick_root=$(path_get_sidekick_root)
            local user_reminder="$sidekick_root/static-reminder.txt"
            if [ -f "$user_reminder" ]; then
                reminder_text=$(cat "$user_reminder")
                log_debug "Loaded user reminder from $user_reminder"
            fi
        fi

        # Output reminder if we have one
        if [ -n "$reminder_text" ]; then
            echo "$reminder_text"
        else
            log_debug "No static reminder file found"
        fi
    fi

    return 0
}

log_debug "Tracking feature loaded"

     2→# Sidekick Feature: Resume
     3→# Session continuity and snarkification

#!/bin/bash
# Sidekick Feature: Resume
# Session continuity and snarkification

# Prevent double-sourcing
[[ -n "${_SIDEKICK_FEATURE_RESUME_LOADED:-}" ]] && return 0
readonly _SIDEKICK_FEATURE_RESUME_LOADED=1

# This file should be sourced, not executed
if [ "${BASH_SOURCE[0]}" -ef "$0" ]; then
    echo "ERROR: This file should be sourced, not executed" >&2
    exit 1
fi

# Requires lib/common.sh to be loaded
if [[ -z "${_SIDEKICK_COMMON_LOADED:-}" ]]; then
    echo "ERROR: lib/common.sh must be loaded before resume.sh" >&2
    exit 1
fi

#------------------------------------------------------------------------------
# resume_snarkify - Generate resume topic from previous session
#
# Finds the most recent previous session with good clarity and generates
# a snarky resume message for the new session
#
# Arguments:
#   $1 - session_id (current session)
#   $2 - project_dir
#
# Returns:
#   0 on success or skip, 1 on failure
#------------------------------------------------------------------------------
resume_snarkify() {
    local session_id="$1"
    local project_dir="$2"

    if [ -z "$session_id" ] || [ -z "$project_dir" ]; then
        log_error "resume_snarkify: session_id and project_dir are required"
        return 1
    fi

    log_info "Starting resume snarkify for session: $session_id"

    # Get session directory
    local session_dir
    session_dir=$(path_get_session_dir "$session_id")

    local current_topic="$session_dir/topic.json"

    # Skip if current topic already exists
    if [ -f "$current_topic" ]; then
        log_debug "Current session topic already exists, skipping resume"
        return 0
    fi

    # Get sessions directory (requires CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR)
    if [ -z "${CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR:-}" ]; then
        log_debug "CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR not set, cannot resume"
        return 0
    fi

    local sessions_dir="${CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR}/.sidekick/sessions"

    log_debug "Searching for previous session in: $sessions_dir"

    # Ensure sessions directory exists
    if [ ! -d "$sessions_dir" ]; then
        log_debug "No sessions directory found, cannot resume"
        return 0
    fi

    # Get configuration
    local min_clarity resume_model
    min_clarity=$(config_get "RESUME_MIN_CLARITY")
    resume_model=$(config_get "RESUME_MODEL")

    # Defaults
    [ -z "$min_clarity" ] && min_clarity=5
    [ -z "$resume_model" ] && resume_model="haiku-4.5"

    log_debug "Resume config: min_clarity=$min_clarity, model=$resume_model"

    # Find most recent topic file with clarity > threshold from OTHER sessions
    # Search all session directories except current one
    local last_topic
    last_topic=$(find "$sessions_dir" -mindepth 1 -maxdepth 1 -type d 2>/dev/null | \
        grep -v "/${session_id}$" | \
        while read -r dir; do
            local topic_file="$dir/topic.json"
            [ -f "$topic_file" ] || continue
            echo "$topic_file"
        done | \
        while read -r file; do
            local clarity timestamp
            clarity=$(jq -r '.clarity_score // 0' "$file" 2>/dev/null || echo "0")
            timestamp=$(jq -r '.timestamp // ""' "$file" 2>/dev/null || echo "")
            # Use file modification time if timestamp is missing
            if [ -z "$timestamp" ]; then
                timestamp=$(stat -c %Y "$file" 2>/dev/null || stat -f %m "$file" 2>/dev/null || echo "0")
            fi
            if [ "$clarity" -gt "$min_clarity" ] && [ -n "$timestamp" ]; then
                echo "${timestamp}|${file}"
            fi
        done | sort -r | head -1 | cut -d'|' -f2)

    if [ -z "$last_topic" ] || [ ! -f "$last_topic" ]; then
        log_debug "No previous session found with clarity > $min_clarity"
        return 0
    fi

    log_info "Found previous session topic: $last_topic"

    # Load prompt template
    local prompt_template="$sidekick_root/features/prompts/new-session-topic.txt"
    if [ ! -f "$prompt_template" ]; then
        log_warn "Prompt template not found: $prompt_template, using fallback"
        _resume_fallback "$session_id" "$last_topic" "$current_topic"
        return 0
    fi

    # Read previous topic content
    local prev_topic_content
    if ! prev_topic_content=$(cat "$last_topic" 2>/dev/null); then
        log_error "Failed to read previous topic file: $last_topic"
        return 1
    fi

    # Build analysis prompt by substituting previous topic
    local analysis_prompt
    analysis_prompt=$(cat "$prompt_template")
    analysis_prompt="${analysis_prompt/\{PREVIOUS_TOPIC\}/$prev_topic_content}"

    log_debug "Calling LLM for resume snarkification (model: $resume_model)"

    # Call Claude for analysis
    local llm_output
    if ! llm_output=$(claude_invoke "$resume_model" "$analysis_prompt" 30); then
        log_warn "LLM invocation failed, using fallback"
        _resume_fallback "$session_id" "$last_topic" "$current_topic"
        return 0
    fi

    log_debug "LLM output received, extracting JSON"

    # Extract JSON from output
    local llm_json
    if ! llm_json=$(claude_extract_json "$llm_output"); then
        log_warn "Failed to extract JSON from LLM output, using fallback"
        _resume_fallback "$session_id" "$last_topic" "$current_topic"
        return 0
    fi

    # Validate JSON
    if ! echo "$llm_json" | jq empty 2>/dev/null; then
        log_warn "Invalid JSON from LLM, using fallback"
        _resume_fallback "$session_id" "$last_topic" "$current_topic"
        return 0
    fi

    # Extract fields from LLM response
    local initial_goal current_objective snark
    initial_goal=$(echo "$llm_json" | jq -r '.initial_goal // ""')
    current_objective=$(echo "$llm_json" | jq -r '.current_objective // ""')
    snark=$(echo "$llm_json" | jq -r '.snarky_comment // ""')

    # Validate we got values
    if [ -z "$initial_goal" ] || [ -z "$current_objective" ]; then
        log_warn "Missing required fields from LLM response, using fallback"
        _resume_fallback "$session_id" "$last_topic" "$current_topic"
        return 0
    fi

    # Create resume topic JSON
    cat > "$current_topic" <<EOF
{
  "session_id": "$session_id",
  "timestamp": "$(date -u +"%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%SZ")",
  "task_ids": [],
  "initial_goal": "$initial_goal",
  "current_objective": "$current_objective",
  "intent_category": "development",
  "clarity_score": 7,
  "confidence": 0.8,
  "snarky_comment": "$snark",
  "resume_from_session": true
}
EOF

    log_info "Created resume topic file: $current_topic"
    return 0
}

#------------------------------------------------------------------------------
# _resume_fallback - Create fallback resume topic
#
# Internal function to create a simple resume topic when LLM fails
#
# Arguments:
#   $1 - session_id
#   $2 - last_topic_file (path to previous topic)
#   $3 - current_topic_file (path to write to)
#
# Returns:
#   0 on success, 1 on failure
#------------------------------------------------------------------------------
_resume_fallback() {
    local session_id="$1"
    local last_topic="$2"
    local current_topic="$3"

    log_debug "Using fallback resume topic creation"

    # Extract previous goal
    local last_goal
    last_goal=$(jq -r '.current_objective // .initial_goal // "previous work"' "$last_topic" 2>/dev/null || echo "previous work")

    # Create fallback topic
    cat > "$current_topic" <<EOF
{
  "session_id": "$session_id",
  "timestamp": "$(date -u +"%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%SZ")",
  "task_ids": [],
  "initial_goal": "Shall we resume ${last_goal}?",
  "current_objective": "Navigate the space-time continuum of code",
  "intent_category": "development",
  "clarity_score": 6,
  "confidence": 0.7,
  "snarky_comment": "Back to work, because apparently we weren't done the first time",
  "resume_from_session": true
}
EOF

    log_info "Created fallback resume topic: $current_topic"
    return 0
}

log_debug "Resume feature loaded"

1→#!/bin/bash
     2→# Sidekick Feature: Cleanup
     3→# Session directory garbage collection
     4→
     5→# Prevent double-sourcing
     6→[[ -n "${_SIDEKICK_FEATURE_CLEANUP_LOADED:-}" ]] && return 0
     7→readonly _SIDEKICK_FEATURE_CLEANUP_LOADED=1
     8→
     9→# This file should be sourced, not executed
    10→if [ "${BASH_SOURCE[0]}" -ef "$0" ]; then
    11→    echo "ERROR: This file should be sourced, not executed" >&2
    12→    exit 1
    13→fi
    14→
    15→# Requires lib/common.sh to be loaded
    16→if [[ -z "${_SIDEKICK_COMMON_LOADED:-}" ]]; then
    17→    echo "ERROR: lib/common.sh must be loaded before cleanup.sh" >&2
    18→    exit 1
    19→fi
    20→
    21→#------------------------------------------------------------------------------
    22→# cleanup_launch - Launch cleanup process in background
    23→#
    24→# Launches the cleanup_run function as a background process with PID tracking
    25→#
    26→# Arguments:
    27→#   $1 - session_id
    28→#   $2 - project_dir
    29→#
    30→# Returns:
    31→#   0 on success, 1 on failure
    32→#------------------------------------------------------------------------------
    33→cleanup_launch() {
    34→    local session_id="$1"
    35→    local project_dir="$2"
    36→
    37→    if [ -z "$session_id" ] || [ -z "$project_dir" ]; then
    38→        log_error "cleanup_launch: session_id and project_dir are required"
    39→        return 1
    40→    fi
    41→
    42→    # Check if cleanup is enabled
    43→    if ! config_is_feature_enabled "cleanup"; then
    44→        log_debug "Cleanup feature is disabled"
    45→        return 0
    46→    fi
    47→
    48→    local cleanup_enabled
    49→    cleanup_enabled=$(config_get "CLEANUP_ENABLED")
    50→    if [ "$cleanup_enabled" != "true" ]; then
    51→        log_debug "Cleanup is disabled (CLEANUP_ENABLED=$cleanup_enabled)"
    52→        return 0
    53→    fi
    54→
    55→    log_info "Launching cleanup process in background"
    56→
    57→    # Launch cleanup_run as background process
    58→    # Export necessary vars for subprocess
    59→    export SIDEKICK_ROOT
    60→    export LOG_LEVEL
    61→    SIDEKICK_ROOT=$(path_get_sidekick_root)
    62→
    63→    # Launch the cleanup process
    64→    # We need to source this file in the subprocess so it has the cleanup_run function
    65→    local session_dir
    66→    session_dir=$(path_get_session_dir "$session_id")
    67→    local pid_file="${session_dir}/cleanup.pid"
    68→    local log_file="${session_dir}/cleanup.log"
    69→
    70→    # Check if already running
    71→    if process_is_running "$pid_file"; then
    72→        log_debug "Cleanup process already running for session ${session_id}"
    73→        return 0
    74→    fi
    75→
    76→    # Launch background process directly (don't use process_launch_background to avoid double bash -c wrapping)
    77→    log_debug "Launching cleanup process for session ${session_id}"
    78→
    79→    nohup bash -c "
    80→        # Source the common library and this feature in the subprocess
    81→        source '$SIDEKICK_ROOT/lib/common.sh'
    82→        source '$SIDEKICK_ROOT/features/cleanup.sh'
    83→
    84→        # Re-load config
    85→        config_load
    86→
    87→        # Re-initialize logging for subprocess
    88→        log_init '$session_id'
    89→
    90→        # Run cleanup
    91→        cleanup_run '$project_dir'
    92→    " > /dev/null 2>&1 &
    93→
    94→    local pid=$!
    95→    echo "$pid" > "$pid_file"
    96→
    97→    log_debug "Launched cleanup with PID ${pid}"
    98→    log_debug "Cleanup process launched with PID file"
    99→
   100→    return 0
   101→}
   102→
   103→#------------------------------------------------------------------------------
   104→# cleanup_run - Main cleanup logic
   105→#
   106→# Performs garbage collection on old session directories
   107→# This function is called in the background process
   108→#
   109→# Arguments:
   110→#   $1 - project_dir (to locate sessions directory)
   111→#
   112→# Returns:
   113→#   0 on success, 1 on failure
   114→#------------------------------------------------------------------------------
   115→cleanup_run() {
   116→    local project_dir="$1"
   117→
   118→    if [ -z "$project_dir" ]; then
   119→        log_error "cleanup_run: project_dir is required"
   120→        return 1
   121→    fi
   122→
   123→    log_info "Starting cleanup process"
   124→
   125→    # Get configuration
   126→    local min_count age_days dry_run
   127→    min_count=$(config_get "CLEANUP_MIN_COUNT")
   128→    age_days=$(config_get "CLEANUP_AGE_DAYS")
   129→    dry_run=$(config_get "CLEANUP_DRY_RUN")
   130→
   131→    # Defaults
   132→    [ -z "$min_count" ] && min_count=5
   133→    [ -z "$age_days" ] && age_days=2
   134→    [ -z "$dry_run" ] && dry_run=false
   135→
   136→    log_debug "Cleanup config: min_count=$min_count, age_days=$age_days, dry_run=$dry_run"
   137→
   138→    # Determine sessions directory path (always at project root)
   139→    local sessions_dir="${project_dir}/.sidekick/sessions"
   140→
   141→    log_debug "Scanning sessions directory: $sessions_dir"
   142→
   143→    # Verify sessions directory exists
   144→    if [ ! -d "$sessions_dir" ]; then
   145→        log_debug "No sessions directory found at: $sessions_dir"
   146→        return 0
   147→    fi
   148→
   149→    # Safety check - must be under .sidekick/sessions
   150→    if [[ ! "$sessions_dir" =~ /.sidekick/sessions$ ]]; then
   151→        log_error "Invalid sessions_dir path (safety check failed): $sessions_dir"
   152→        return 1
   153→    fi
   154→
   155→    # Find all session directories matching UUID pattern
   156→    # UUID format: 8-4-4-4-12 hex digits
   157→    local uuid_pattern="[0-9a-f]{8}-[0-9a-f]{4}-[0-9a-f]{4}-[0-9a-f]{4}-[0-9a-f]{12}"
   158→    local session_dirs=()
   159→
   160→    while IFS= read -r -d '' dir; do
   161→        local basename

#!/bin/bash
# Sidekick Feature: Cleanup
# Session directory garbage collection

# Prevent double-sourcing
[[ -n "${_SIDEKICK_FEATURE_CLEANUP_LOADED:-}" ]] && return 0
readonly _SIDEKICK_FEATURE_CLEANUP_LOADED=1

# This file should be sourced, not executed
if [ "${BASH_SOURCE[0]}" -ef "$0" ]; then
    echo "ERROR: This file should be sourced, not executed" >&2
    exit 1
fi

# Requires lib/common.sh to be loaded
if [[ -z "${_SIDEKICK_COMMON_LOADED:-}" ]]; then
    echo "ERROR: lib/common.sh must be loaded before cleanup.sh" >&2
    exit 1
fi

#------------------------------------------------------------------------------
# cleanup_launch - Launch cleanup process in background
#
# Launches the cleanup_run function as a background process with PID tracking
#
# Arguments:
#   $1 - session_id
#   $2 - project_dir
#
# Returns:
#   0 on success, 1 on failure
#------------------------------------------------------------------------------
cleanup_launch() {
    local session_id="$1"
    local project_dir="$2"

    if [ -z "$session_id" ] || [ -z "$project_dir" ]; then
        log_error "cleanup_launch: session_id and project_dir are required"
        return 1
    fi

    # Check if cleanup is enabled
    if ! config_is_feature_enabled "cleanup"; then
        log_debug "Cleanup feature is disabled"
        return 0
    fi

    local cleanup_enabled
    cleanup_enabled=$(config_get "CLEANUP_ENABLED")
    if [ "$cleanup_enabled" != "true" ]; then
        log_debug "Cleanup is disabled (CLEANUP_ENABLED=$cleanup_enabled)"
        return 0
    fi

    log_info "Launching cleanup process in background"

    # Launch cleanup_run as background process
    # Export necessary vars for subprocess
    export SIDEKICK_ROOT
    export LOG_LEVEL
    SIDEKICK_ROOT=$(path_get_sidekick_root)

    # Launch the cleanup process
    # We need to source this file in the subprocess so it has the cleanup_run function
    local session_dir
    session_dir=$(path_get_session_dir "$session_id")
    local pid_file="${session_dir}/cleanup.pid"
    local log_file="${session_dir}/cleanup.log"

    # Check if already running
    if process_is_running "$pid_file"; then
        log_debug "Cleanup process already running for session ${session_id}"
        return 0
    fi

    # Launch background process directly (don't use process_launch_background to avoid double bash -c wrapping)
    log_debug "Launching cleanup process for session ${session_id}"

    nohup bash -c "
        # Source the common library and this feature in the subprocess
        source '$SIDEKICK_ROOT/lib/common.sh'
        source '$SIDEKICK_ROOT/features/cleanup.sh'

        # Re-load config
        config_load

        # Re-initialize logging for subprocess
        log_init '$session_id'

        # Run cleanup
        cleanup_run '$project_dir'
    " > /dev/null 2>&1 &

    local pid=$!
    echo "$pid" > "$pid_file"

    log_debug "Launched cleanup with PID ${pid}"
    log_debug "Cleanup process launched with PID file"

    return 0
}

#------------------------------------------------------------------------------
# cleanup_run - Main cleanup logic
#
# Performs garbage collection on old session directories
# This function is called in the background process
#
# Arguments:
#   $1 - project_dir (to locate sessions directory)
#
# Returns:
#   0 on success, 1 on failure
#------------------------------------------------------------------------------
cleanup_run() {
    local project_dir="$1"

    if [ -z "$project_dir" ]; then
        log_error "cleanup_run: project_dir is required"
        return 1
    fi

    log_info "Starting cleanup process"

    # Get configuration
    local min_count age_days dry_run
    min_count=$(config_get "CLEANUP_MIN_COUNT")
    age_days=$(config_get "CLEANUP_AGE_DAYS")
    dry_run=$(config_get "CLEANUP_DRY_RUN")

    # Defaults
    [ -z "$min_count" ] && min_count=5
    [ -z "$age_days" ] && age_days=2
    [ -z "$dry_run" ] && dry_run=false

    log_debug "Cleanup config: min_count=$min_count, age_days=$age_days, dry_run=$dry_run"

    # Determine sessions directory path (always at project root)
    local sessions_dir="${project_dir}/.sidekick/sessions"

    log_debug "Scanning sessions directory: $sessions_dir"

    # Verify sessions directory exists
    if [ ! -d "$sessions_dir" ]; then
        log_debug "No sessions directory found at: $sessions_dir"
        return 0
    fi

    # Safety check - must be under .sidekick/sessions
    if [[ ! "$sessions_dir" =~ /.sidekick/sessions$ ]]; then
        log_error "Invalid sessions_dir path (safety check failed): $sessions_dir"
        return 1
    fi

    # Find all session directories matching UUID pattern
    # UUID format: 8-4-4-4-12 hex digits
    local uuid_pattern="[0-9a-f]{8}-[0-9a-f]{4}-[0-9a-f]{4}-[0-9a-f]{4}-[0-9a-f]{12}"
    local session_dirs=()

    while IFS= read -r -d '' dir; do
        local basename
        basename=$(basename "$dir")
        # Validate UUID pattern for safety
        if [[ "$basename" =~ ^$uuid_pattern$ ]]; then
            session_dirs+=("$dir")
        else
            log_warn "Skipping non-UUID directory: $basename"
        fi
    done < <(find "$sessions_dir" -mindepth 1 -maxdepth 1 -type d -print0 2>/dev/null || true)

    local total_count=${#session_dirs[@]}
    log_debug "Found $total_count session directories"

    if [ $total_count -eq 0 ]; then
        log_debug "No session directories to process"
        return 0
    fi

    # Calculate age for each session directory
    # Age is based on the newest file in the directory
    local current_time
    current_time=$(date +%s)
    local age_threshold_seconds=$((age_days * 24 * 60 * 60))

    declare -A session_ages  # session_dir -> age_in_seconds
    local old_sessions=()

    for session_dir in "${session_dirs[@]}"; do
        # Find newest file in session directory
        # Using -printf '%T@' to get modification time as Unix timestamp
        local newest_file_time
        newest_file_time=$(find "$session_dir" -type f -printf '%T@\n' 2>/dev/null | sort -nr | head -1)

        if [ -z "$newest_file_time" ]; then
            # Empty directory or no files - use directory mtime
            newest_file_time=$(stat -c '%Y' "$session_dir" 2>/dev/null || echo "$current_time")
        fi

        # Convert to integer (truncate fractional seconds)
        newest_file_time=${newest_file_time%.*}

        # Calculate age in seconds
        local age=$((current_time - newest_file_time))
        session_ages["$session_dir"]=$age

        # Filter sessions older than threshold
        if [ $age -ge $age_threshold_seconds ]; then
            old_sessions+=("$session_dir")
            local age_days_actual=$((age / 86400))
            log_debug "Old session: $(basename "$session_dir") (${age_days_actual} days old)"
        fi
    done

    local old_count=${#old_sessions[@]}
    log_info "Found $old_count old sessions (>$age_days days) out of $total_count total"

    # Only cleanup if we exceed the minimum count threshold
    if [ $old_count -le $min_count ]; then
        log_info "Old session count ($old_count) <= threshold ($min_count), no cleanup needed"
        return 0
    fi

    # Sort old sessions by age (oldest first) for deletion
    # We'll remove sessions to bring the old count down to the threshold
    local sessions_to_remove=$((old_count - min_count))
    log_info "Will remove $sessions_to_remove oldest sessions to reach threshold of $min_count"

    # Create array of [age session_dir] for sorting
    local age_dir_pairs=()
    for session_dir in "${old_sessions[@]}"; do
        local age=${session_ages["$session_dir"]}
        age_dir_pairs+=("$age $session_dir")
    done

    # Sort by age (descending) and extract session directories
    IFS=$'\n' local sorted_pairs=($(sort -rn <<<"${age_dir_pairs[*]}"))
    unset IFS

    # Remove the oldest sessions
    local removed_count=0
    for i in $(seq 0 $((sessions_to_remove - 1))); do
        local pair="${sorted_pairs[$i]}"
        local session_dir="${pair#* }"  # Extract session_dir from "age session_dir"
        local age=${session_ages["$session_dir"]}
        local age_days_actual=$((age / 86400))
        local session_id
        session_id=$(basename "$session_dir")

        if [ "$dry_run" = "true" ]; then
            log_info "[DRY-RUN] Would remove: $session_id (${age_days_actual} days old)"
        else
            log_info "Removing: $session_id (${age_days_actual} days old)"
            if rm -rf "$session_dir" 2>/dev/null; then
                removed_count=$((removed_count + 1))
            else
                log_error "Failed to remove: $session_dir"
            fi
        fi
    done

    if [ "$dry_run" = "true" ]; then
        log_info "Dry-run complete: would have removed $sessions_to_remove sessions"
    else
        log_info "Cleanup complete: removed $removed_count of $sessions_to_remove sessions"
    fi

    return 0
}

log_debug "Cleanup feature loaded"

# Parse LLM output and write topic.json
# Args: session_id, llm_output, mode
_topic_extraction_write_topic() {
    local session_id="$1"
    local llm_output="$2"
    local mode="$3"

    local session_dir=$(path_get_session_dir "$session_id")
    local topic_file="${session_dir}/topic.json"

    log_debug "Parsing LLM output for topic extraction"

    # Extract JSON from output (handles markdown wrapping)
    local json_output
    json_output=$(claude_extract_json "$llm_output")

    if ! json_validate "$json_output"; then
        log_error "Invalid JSON from LLM output"
        return 1
    fi

    # For topic-only mode, select appropriate snarky comment based on clarity
    if [ "$mode" = "topic-only" ]; then
        local clarity
        clarity=$(echo "$json_output" | jq -r '.clarity_score // 5')

        local snarky_comment
        if [ "$clarity" -ge 7 ] 2>/dev/null; then
            snarky_comment=$(echo "$json_output" | jq -r '.high_clarity_snarky_comment // ""')
